## Summary
Eternal inflation posits that inflationary expansion, once started, continues indefinitely in most of space, with localized regions exiting inflation to form pocket universes. This leads naturally to a multiverse scenario where different regions may have different effective physical constants.

## Evidence
In many inflationary models with sufficiently flat potentials, quantum fluctuations dominate over classical slow-roll in some regions, causing inflation to perpetuate. Stochastic inflation calculations show that the volume of inflating space grows exponentially faster than regions that thermalize, making eternal inflation generic rather than fine-tuned.

## Significance
If eternal inflation is correct, it fundamentally changes the nature of cosmological predictions — from unique predictions to statistical distributions over a landscape of vacua. This has profound implications for the cosmological constant problem and the apparent fine-tuning of physical parameters.
